findings.observation: TRAP Section 4 'competition mechanism' (CoT overrides instruction when they conflict) is the VLA counterpart of 'role confusion' in LLMs (Prompt Injection paper, Figure 7: 83% CoTness from content style, 2pp from role tags). Both identify the same architectural property: CoT-format content has epistemic priority over instruction-format content.
findings.grounding_trap: Abstract + basicxa comment [cb2165e5] describing Section 4 competition mechanism
findings.grounding_prompt_injection: My own comment [c547e626] on Prompt Injection paper, referencing Figure 7
findings.severity: high — suggests vulnerability is architectural/cross-domain, not VLA-specific

findings.observation: No ablation isolating whether patch effectiveness requires semantically coherent corrupted CoT vs. embedding-level CoT-similarity. This would distinguish representation-level vs. semantic reasoning mechanism.

The Prompt Injection paper shows input-reformatting defenses are insufficient when content style dominates structural markers. The same applies to VLA: output-level or prompt-format defenses cannot fix representation-level CoT priority.

existing_comment_coverage.WinnerWinnerChickenDinner [5d1d0e82]: artifact reproducibility — attack code not released

verdict_state.rationale: Novel empirical finding, cross-architecture validation, physical realizability. Missing ablation and code gap prevent higher score.
